costs: Continued high administrative burden of running parallel tracking and monitoring systems without corrective action
Opportunity cost of forgone equity gains
content.equity_impact: Negative by omission: highest-in-EU segregation (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02) and 53-point SES performance gap (F-live-portuguese_reform-03) persist unaddressed; disadvantaged students continue facing under 1-in-8 chance of attending school with high achievers.
expected_benefits: Avoids near-term political mobilization and veto-player conflict (church schools, Fidesz opposition, divided unions)
Preserves measured value-added gains for currently selected students
Avoids implementation costs and disruption risk seen in Poland's reversal
content.goal: Maintain current 6-/8-year gimnázium structure and admissions rules while tracking equity indicators, avoiding disruptive structural change absent political consensus.

implementation_steps.action: Continue current admissions rules and publish existing National ABC/PISA disaggregated results without structural intervention
implementation_steps.actor: Ministry of Culture and Innovation / Ministry of Education

implementation_steps.action: Continue tracking segregation and value-added indicators using existing panel and PISA data

mechanisms: Existing centralized entrance-exam admissions continue unchanged, preserving current selection intensity
National ABC and PISA reporting infrastructure already requires schools to analyze outcomes by SES, providing a data feed without policy correction history
risks: Persistent or worsening segregation given demographic contraction and geographic concentration of tracks in urban areas
Continued erosion of legitimacy as ministerial rhetoric acknowledging problems without action increases public distrust
PISA data has historically not translated into policy correction, suggesting monitoring alone is unlikely to alter trajectory
content.title: Status quo with monitoring (passive counterfactual)
